chunks.heading: INTRODUCTION
chunks.heading_path_str: 36. wwH Working Women Hostel > INTRODUCTION
chunks.text: The Government of India enacted various legislations, namely, the protection of women from Domestic violence Act, 2005, The lmmor.l Traffic iprevention) Act, 1956, the Dowry Prohibition Act 1961, the prohibition of child Marriage Act, ioo6, for women in need of protection, care, safety and security while iri distress for catering to their basic needs through proper ca-re, protection, d.evelopment, treatment and social re-integration. Despite these legislative interventions, t-here is a,' urgent need to provide women with institutional help either in distress circumsta,ces or provide them enabiing environment for ivail benefrts offered under various schemes launched by the central Government or state Government aimed for her betterment. During the XV Finance commission period, the Ministry of women & child Development has launched Mission Shakti for the overarl development of the women.
chunks.contextualized_text: 36. wwH Working Women Hostel > INTRODUCTION The Government of India enacted various legislations, namely, the protection of women from Domestic violence Act, 2005, The lmmor.l Traffic iprevention) Act, 1956, the Dowry Prohibition Act 1961, the prohibition of child Marriage Act, ioo6, for women in need of protection, care, safety and security while iri distress for catering to their basic needs through proper ca-re, protection, d.evelopment, treatment and social re-integration. Despite these legislative interventions, t-here is a,' urgent need to provide women with institutional help either in distress circumsta,ces or provide them enabiing environment for ivail benefrts offered under various schemes launched by the central Government or state Government aimed for her betterment. During the XV Finance commission period, the Ministry of women & child Development has launched Mission Shakti for the overarl development of the women.

chunks.text: 6. During the 15th Finarce Commission period, Women Helpline shall be 100% funded under Mission Shalti by the Government of India, for all States/UTs. The funds would be released to State/UT Governments through Public Financial Management System (PFMS). States/UTs shall comply with all the guidelines issued by the Department of Expenditure, Ministry of Finance regarding procedure of release of funds ald aly further protocols on operation of Women Helpline issued from tirne to time by the Ministry. 7. The States/UT shall reflect the amount in the audited statement of accounts together with the necessary Utilization Certificate and Statement of Expenditure in reipect of the grant for Women Heipline and submit the same to the Ministry after close of financial year. 8. The Utilization certificate and Statement of Expenditure in respect of grants received for Women Helpline shatl be furnished in accordance to Rule 239-24O GFR 2Ol7 andin the Form 12c ofGFR 2017. The provision of Rule 241 GFR 2017 shall a,lso be ensured.

chunks.text: The WHL Control Room to be provided with adequate space and infrastructure by the concerned States/UTs. The WHL-CR may function from the premises of the State Depa.rtment working on the women protection/welfare issues in the State Efforts may be made to utilize the existing infrastructure of States/UTs instead of creation of new infrastructure. Space requirement for each State/UT will vary as the number of call taking consoles (No. of workstations) for a State/UT depends on the category. Each CallTaking console requires 30 sq.ft [6x5) and the Data Centre requires 10O sq. ft The data centre facility envisaged is common for WHL and CHL operations and there shall be no separate sanction for physical infrastructurer building, machines etc. for WIIL or CHL. For the optimum and efficient use of available resources, it is mandated that WIIL and CHL Call-Takers are co-located in the same venue (WHL-CRI. The Data Centre will be setup in the WHL Centre. In any case if the CI{L Call-Takers sit at a dlffereat locatlon' additional dedicated MPLSI connectivity would be arranged between WCD CR and CHL unit the State UT. A11 incoming calls to 181 may be classified into following categories:

chunks.heading: A. Women Helpline Unit at One Stop Centres (OSCs):
heading_path: A. Women Helpline Unit at One Stop Centres (OSCs):
chunks.heading_path_str: 4. WOMEN HELPLINE I'NIT (CHLI AT OSCS > A. Women Helpline Unit at One Stop Centres (OSCs):
chunks.text: The one Stop centre working under the overall supervision of District Magistrate is the nodal agency for ensuring service delivery and care and protection of women in the district. The OSC shall implement all women protection legislations, schemes ald work for achievement of women protection goals as laid out in the Mission Shakti scheme guidelines. The central Administrator working under the overall administrative control of District Magistrate sha-lI be available round the clock and provide outreach service for women in crisis linking them to emergency and long-term care and rehabilitation services. Each of this unit shall abide by the provisions as laid down under Mission Shakti guidelines while handling cases of women. All efforts should be made to provide space for WHL Unit in the existing OSCs for better coordination and monitoring with proper security arrangement. The existing manpower of OSC shall ensure that none of the calls forwarded by the WHL should remain unanswered.
chunks.contextualized_text: 4. WOMEN HELPLINE I'NIT (CHLI AT OSCS > A. Women Helpline Unit at One Stop Centres (OSCs): The one Stop centre working under the overall supervision of District Magistrate is the nodal agency for ensuring service delivery and care and protection of women in the district. The OSC shall implement all women protection legislations, schemes ald work for achievement of women protection goals as laid out in the Mission Shakti scheme guidelines. The central Administrator working under the overall administrative control of District Magistrate sha-lI be available round the clock and provide outreach service for women in crisis linking them to emergency and long-term care and rehabilitation services. Each of this unit shall abide by the provisions as laid down under Mission Shakti guidelines while handling cases of women. All efforts should be made to provide space for WHL Unit in the existing OSCs for better coordination and monitoring with proper security arrangement. The existing manpower of OSC shall ensure that none of the calls forwarded by the WHL should remain unanswered.

chunks.heading: Roles and Responsibilities of OSC:
heading_path: A. Women Helpline Unit at One Stop Centres (OSCs):
chunks.heading_path_str: 4. WOMEN HELPLINE I'NIT (CHLI AT OSCS > A. Women Helpline Unit at One Stop Centres (OSCs): > Roles and Responsibilities of OSC:
chunks.text: i) Responding to calls related to women which have been transferred from WHL Control Room and provision of rescue and outreach services for women in need of care and protection; ii) Reaching the woman in distress requiring physical intervention is expected to be completed within 3O minutes on €rn average; iii) Coordinating rescue and other outreach services with the help of relevant local departments like police, administration, health, and others; iv) Ensuring proper documentation of all women rescued to facilitate their rehabilitation ald restoration;

chunks.heading: WHL Control Room as per the time lines given below:
heading_path: WHL Control Room as per the time lines given below:
chunks.heading_path_str: 4.3 Call Management: > WHL Control Room as per the time lines given below:
chunks.text: In emergency cases i) First follow-up to be done within t hour of transferring the case' ii) Second follow-up within 8 hours of transferring the case. iii) Third fol1ow-up to be done within 24 hours of transferring the case. iv) Subsequent foliow-up after every 7 days from 1s day of case handover till case is marked as closed by the WHL unit. In non-emergency cases v) Follow-up to be after 24 hours of providing the information to the caller seeking information about Government Schemes. vi) Subsequent follow-up after every 7 days from 1$ day of case haldover ti1l case is marked as closed by the WHL unit. OSC will send a daily report to the WHL Control Room regarding transferred cases to the WHL unit.

chunks.heading: management:
chunks.heading_path_str: 4.3 Call Management: > management:
chunks.text: All States/UTs must make concerted efforts to work closely with ail a-llied departments like Education, Health, Home/Police, hospitals etc., in order to ensure convergence, support and cooperation during case intervention. This takes place through four main areas of collaboration: (a) case intervention (rescue, restoration with families, access to entitlements and rehabilitation); (b) orientation capacity building of allied departments to share understarding towards more effective interventions mentioned in (a); (c) co-organizing with state and district administration special campaigns, events on days of significance, awareness drives, facilitating access of schemes, (d) coordination and convergence from micro to macro level, a continued work in progress towards better women friendly services, timely effective rehabilitation and access to justice. The feedback and findings of women protection issues intervened by WHL cR/oSCs should be collated and shared as and when asked by the central state Government/ UT Administration. (i) In case of requirement of police or medical assistance, ca-ll shall be trarsferred to ERSS- 1 12 to provide all possible assistance to the caller.

This takes place through four main areas of collaboration: (a) case intervention (rescue, restoration with families, access to entitlements and rehabilitation); (b) orientation capacity building of allied departments to share understarding towards more effective interventions mentioned in (a); (c) co-organizing with state and district administration special campaigns, events on days of significance, awareness drives, facilitating access of schemes, (d) coordination and convergence from micro to macro level, a continued work in progress towards better women friendly services, timely effective rehabilitation and access to justice. The feedback and findings of women protection issues intervened by WHL cR/oSCs should be collated and shared as and when asked by the central state Government/ UT Administration. (i) In case of requirement of police or medical assistance, ca-ll shall be trarsferred to ERSS- 1 12 to provide all possible assistance to the caller.

chunks.heading: 6. TRAININGAND CAPACITY BUILDING
chunks.heading_path_str: 6. TRAININGAND CAPACITY BUILDING
chunks.text: C-DAC will provide technical training including halds-on training at the WHL Control Room/SHEW/DHEW where WHL/CHL/OSC personnel to work on the system and to familiarize the operations of the system. Manuals required for operations will be provided by C-DAC. State/UT sha-Il extend all support to C-DAC for conducting the training. Ministry will also collaborate with C-DAC, Nationa-l Institute of Public Cooperatron and Child Development (NIPCCD), and Nationa-l Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences (NIMHANS) for capacity building of staff, developing of intervention protocols/training modules for effective impiementation of t1 e Mission Shakti schemes and for providing psycho-social support to women during calls and physical interventions. Ministry w'ilt also support outreach strategies to universalize access and use of the Women Helpline in the States/UTs.
chunks.contextualized_text: 6. TRAININGAND CAPACITY BUILDING C-DAC will provide technical training including halds-on training at the WHL Control Room/SHEW/DHEW where WHL/CHL/OSC personnel to work on the system and to familiarize the operations of the system. Manuals required for operations will be provided by C-DAC. State/UT sha-Il extend all support to C-DAC for conducting the training. Ministry will also collaborate with C-DAC, Nationa-l Institute of Public Cooperatron and Child Development (NIPCCD), and Nationa-l Institute of Mental Health and Neurosciences (NIMHANS) for capacity building of staff, developing of intervention protocols/training modules for effective impiementation of t1 e Mission Shakti schemes and for providing psycho-social support to women during calls and physical interventions. Ministry w'ilt also support outreach strategies to universalize access and use of the Women Helpline in the States/UTs.

chunks.heading: REPORTINGMECHANISM
chunks.heading_path_str: 6. TRAININGAND CAPACITY BUILDING > REPORTINGMECHANISM
chunks.text: G-DAC shall develop MIS report generation modules for States/UTs for reviewing of cases and better decision making which may be customized as per specilic needs of the states, if required. An MIS module shall a-lso be prepared for the Ministry for monitoring of wHL at state and district leve1s. This MIS will generate data which would enable policy decision making for the best interest of the women. Reporting module based on following parameters is to be developed by C-DAC: i) State-wise calls related to women landing at 181. ii) State-wise calls landing at 112 forwarded to 181. iii) State-wise calls forwarded from 181 to 112 requiring specialized services of Police, fire and medical. irr) Categorization of calls answered based on providing merely information, advice, psychosocial support or requiring physical intervention and forwarded to OSCs as the case may be. v) Average time taken to close the call. vi) Make Women Helpline data available on Mission Shal<ti dashboard portal.
chunks.contextualized_text: 6. TRAININGAND CAPACITY BUILDING > REPORTINGMECHANISM G-DAC shall develop MIS report generation modules for States/UTs for reviewing of cases and better decision making which may be customized as per specilic needs of the states, if required. An MIS module shall a-lso be prepared for the Ministry for monitoring of wHL at state and district leve1s. This MIS will generate data which would enable policy decision making for the best interest of the women. Reporting module based on following parameters is to be developed by C-DAC: i) State-wise calls related to women landing at 181. ii) State-wise calls landing at 112 forwarded to 181. iii) State-wise calls forwarded from 181 to 112 requiring specialized services of Police, fire and medical. irr) Categorization of calls answered based on providing merely information, advice, psychosocial support or requiring physical intervention and forwarded to OSCs as the case may be. v) Average time taken to close the call. vi) Make Women Helpline data available on Mission Shal<ti dashboard portal.
